description: V5 of chemical conservation facts. Targeted fixes for stubborn failures.

facts.context: Conservation laws in a chemical reaction network are found in:
facts.truth: the left null space of the stoichiometry matrix
distractors: the right null space of the stoichiometry matrix
the column space of the stoichiometry matrix
the row space of the stoichiometry matrix

facts.context: For a reversible reaction A <-> B, the conserved quantity is:
distractors: [A] - [B] = constant (net difference conservation)
[A] / [B] = constant (equilibrium ratio preserved)
[A] * [B] = constant (product coupling invariant)

facts.context: In Michaelis-Menten enzyme kinetics E + S <-> ES -> E + P, the conserved quantities are:
distractors: [E] + [S] = constant (free enzyme plus substrate) and [ES] + [P] = constant (bound complex plus product)
[E] * [S] / [ES] = constant (binding equilibrium ratio) and [S] - [P] = constant (net substrate balance)
[E] + [P] = constant (enzyme plus product pool) and [S] + [ES] - [P] = constant (modified substrate sum)

facts.context: The dimension of the conservation law space equals:
facts.truth: number of species minus rank of stoichiometry matrix
distractors: number of reactions minus rank of stoichiometry matrix
number of complexes minus rank of Jacobian matrix
number of species minus number of reactions

facts.truth: conserved as the total adenine nucleotide pool
distractors: variable depending on the glycolytic flux rate
exchanged with oxidative phosphorylation pools
determined by the cellular energy charge ratio

facts.truth: conserved as the total nicotinamide pool
distractors: variable depending on the glycolytic flux rate
exchanged with mitochondrial electron carriers
determined by the cellular redox potential

facts.context: In open chemical systems with external species exchange:
facts.truth: conservation laws break down due to boundary fluxes
distractors: conservation laws remain valid at all times
new conservation laws arise from boundaries
conservation laws hold at steady state only

facts.context: Under mass-action kinetics, the rate of reaction A + B -> C is:
facts.truth: k * [A] * [B] where k is the rate constant
distractors: k * [C] / ([A] + [B]) as the reverse equilibrium
k * ([A] + [B]) where k is the rate constant
k * [A]^2 * [B]^2 scaled by stoichiometric factors

facts.context: The Lotka-Volterra equations, viewed as a reaction network, have:
distractors: one stoichiometric conservation law
two stoichiometric conservation laws
infinitely many conservation laws

facts.truth: certain linear combinations of intermediate and product concentrations
distractors: the total concentration of all species summed at every time point
the ratio of intermediate to product concentrations at equilibrium
the squared amplitude of oscillations throughout the reaction cycle

facts.context: The deficiency of a chemical reaction network measures:
facts.truth: the difference between complexes minus linkage classes minus stoichiometric rank
distractors: the difference between species minus reactions minus Jacobian rank
the difference between reactions minus linkage classes minus complexes
the difference between stoichiometric rank minus species plus components
each of the fourteen molecular species separately
each of the twelve reaction steps separately
none because glycolysis is thermodynamically open

facts.context: To verify a conservation law w*c = constant:
facts.truth: simulate the system and check that w*c(t) is constant
distractors: compute the Jacobian eigenvalues for stability
evaluate the determinant of the stoichiometry matrix
check that reaction rates balance at t=0

facts.truth: chemical groups that rearrange but are never created or destroyed
distractors: thermodynamic constraints limiting feasible reactions
equilibrium constants from microscopic reversibility
rate limits imposed by enzyme saturation kinetics

facts.truth: all concentrations reachable from an initial condition
distractors: all equilibria achievable with different rate constants
all initial states leading to the same equilibrium
all networks with the same number of conservation laws

facts.context: A non-trivial conservation law in a large network is typically:
facts.truth: a linear combination of many species with non-obvious coefficients
distractors: a pairwise sum of exactly two species with unit weights
a product of concentration ratios forming a dimensionless quantity
a quadratic polynomial of concentrations with integer exponents
